89317696-d6bb-4af3-9d7d-5a58f113ec1b.title: Visit https://github.com/python/cpython and extract repository metadata

details.intent: Directly visit the CPython GitHub repository page (mandated) and extract the requested metadata from the page itself (do not rely on search results).

extraction_plan.primary_programming_language: Read the repository language summary (language label or colored language bar) usually shown near the top-right or under the repo file listing. If multiple languages listed, choose the top/primary language shown by GitHub.
extraction_plan.stars_count_approximate: Extract the visible stargazers count from the 'Stars' button on the repo page (may be abbreviated, e.g., '46.7k'). Record the exact string shown on the page and also provide a numeric approximation if possible.
extraction_plan.license_type: Locate the 'License' label in the repository metadata area (often in the repo header/sidebar) or open the LICENSE file link if visible inline. Extract the license short name (e.g., 'Python-2.0', 'PSF', 'MIT') as displayed.
extraction_plan.brief_description: Extract the short repository description displayed under the repository name/header (one-sentence summary).
extraction_plan.page_url: Confirm and record the exact visited URL.

verification_steps: Capture the repository full name (should be 'python/cpython') from the page header to ensure we are on the correct repo.
Record the exact text of the stars button and the license label as displayed (these are verifiable strings).
If license is not visible in metadata, check file listing for a LICENSE or COPYING file link and extract license name from that file header or content snippet.
If language bar lists multiple languages, use the first/primary language shown and record the full language breakdown text as extra evidence.
details.reasoning_rationale: The mandate requires a direct visit to the GitHub page. This visit node will produce the raw evidence (exact strings and page URL) needed to answer the four requested items reliably. It reduces uncertainty by extracting verifiable page elements rather than relying on third-party summaries.
details.vector_db_note: Chroma/links collections will be auto-queried for related links; primary evidence comes from the direct page content returned by this visit.
